texto: He reasoned, even as a young man, that traditions may linger as he walked though the oracles of time. In later years he thought his mind may one day blur, should he survive to an old age, but as he spread ink on paper, transmitted and shared with those who came after him his experiences, his own great adventures, he believed perhaps they, like he, would give way to pause to reflect on how...hard it always was to open his eyes to begin a new day...
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Andrew Coster: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “He reasoned, even as a young man, that traditions may linger as he walked though the oracles of t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, adventure, bangkok pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bangkok e crime.

frase_original: He reasoned, even as a young man, that traditions may linger as he walked though the oracles of time. In later years he thought his mind may one day blur, should he survive to an old age, but as he spread ink on paper, transmitted and shared with those who came after him his experiences, his own great adventures, he believed perhaps they, like he, would give way to pause to reflect on how...hard it always was to open his eyes to begin a new day...
